summary: ANDROID 🟥 Correct Firmware version for Charge Case but Update available on Software tab after DFU
scenario: DFU update button remains available with same build number after Charge Case firmware update on Voyager Legend 50, causing immediate failure on re-tap until app restart
description_clean: After performing a DFU (Device Firmware Update) on the Charge Case for Voyager Legend 50, the correct firmware version is displayed on the Software tab, but the "Update" button remains available with the same build number. Tapping the Update button again causes the DFU to fail immediately. The issue is resolved after restarting the app, which then shows the proper firmware version.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Navigate to Home screen and connect to Voyager Legend 50 device.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Voyager Legend 50] → Software tab.
4. Verify the currently displayed firmware version for the Charge Case.
5. Perform a Device Firmware Update (DFU) on the Charge Case via the app.
6. After the DFU completes successfully, return to the Software tab.
7. Observe that the correct (updated) firmware version is displayed.
8. Check if the "Update" button is still visible and shows the same build number as before the update.
expected: After a successful DFU, the Software tab should display the new firmware version and the "Update" button should no longer be available (or should show the new build number). Tapping the "Update" button again should either be disabled or initiate a valid update to a newer version.
actual: The correct firmware version is displayed on the Software tab, but the "Update" button remains available with the same old build number. Tapping the "Update" button again causes the DFU to fail immediately. The issue is resolved only after restarting the app, which then shows the proper firmware version and removes the stale "Update" button.
